text: The surgeon performs ligation of the inferior mesenteric artery and mesorectal dissection using a medial to lateral approach. The splenic flexure is fully mobilized, followed by an anterior resection with total mesorectal excision. The proximal sigmoid colon and mesocolon are transected using endoGI staplers.

After confirming the adequate length of the proximal colon, the surgeon introduces a circular stapler through the anus to the end of the rectal stump. The stapler is opened, and a suture is placed around the gap between the stapler head and the anvil head, securing the rectal stump to the circular stapler for withdrawal.

The surgeon uses traction on the circular stapler to fully evert the rectal stump through the anus. Distal transection is performed at the level of the pectineal line, continuing until the abdominal plane of dissection is reached.
The surgeon removes the total mesorectal excision (TME) specimen. The left colonic remnant is extracted through the anus and opened for inspection.
The surgeon performs a hand-sewn coloanal anastomosis using interrupted absorbable sutures, with the surgical field held open by a Lone Star retractor.
The surgeon reviews the colonic position within the abdominal cavity using laparoscopic visualization. A diverting ileostomy is visible, indicating the completion of the procedure.
